_comment: All Sakyadhita International Conferences, newest first. Each becomes a page at /conferences/<n>. Photos: 'f' is an image path or URL, 'yt' is a YouTube video id. 'coverage' can be one item or a list. To add conference #21: copy the top block and edit.

conferences.title: Sakyadhita International Conference on Buddhist Women in Indonesia
conferences.about: The 20th Sakyadhita International Conference travels to Indonesia — bringing together Buddhist women from across the world's traditions for five days of teachings, scholarship, and friendship. Hosted by the Indonesian Buddhist Association, the program will feature plenary lectures, panel discussions, meditation sessions, cultural performances, and opportunities to connect with practitioners and scholars from over 30 countries. We look forward to welcoming you to Indonesia in June 2027.

conferences.title: Navigating Change: Buddhist Women in Transition
conferences.subtitle: Adapting Across Personal, Social, and Spiritual Worlds
conferences.about: The 19th Sakyadhita International Conference gathered Buddhist women from across the world in Kuching, the capital of Sarawak in East Malaysia. Over nine days, participants explored how Buddhist women navigate change — personal, social, and spiritual — in a rapidly transforming world. Hosted by the Buddhist Society of Sarawak, the program featured plenary lectures, paper presentations, meditation sessions, and cultural performances celebrating the rich Buddhist heritage of Borneo.

coverage.headline: Buddhist Women Gather to Discuss Ordination, Social Justice, and Solidarity
coverage.excerpt: The 19th Sakyadhita Conference brought together 400 participants from twenty-five countries in Kuching, Malaysia, to address issues facing Buddhist women. Photos by Olivier Adam.

video.title: Highlights of the 19th Sakyadhita Conference in Sarawak, East Malaysia.
video.caption: SARAWAK · JUNE 2025

conferences.title: Living in a Precarious World
conferences.subtitle: Impermanence, Resilience, Awakening
conferences.about: The largest gathering in Sakyadhita's history. More than 3,000 Buddhist monastics, laypeople, and dignitaries from over 30 countries convened in Seoul to explore impermanence, resilience, and awakening in a world reshaped by pandemic, conflict, and climate crisis. Held at the Coex Convention Center in Gangnam, the five-day program featured plenary lectures, workshops, cultural performances, and a temple stay program at historic Korean monasteries.

coverage.headline: Daughters of the Buddha: 18th Sakyadhita Conference in Seoul Celebrates the Sacred Feminine
coverage.excerpt: Buddhist monastics and laywomen from around the world gathered in Seoul for the largest conference in Sakyadhita's history — a shining example of the alignment between Buddhist scholarship, activism, and practice. Photos by Olivier Adam.

photos.caption: Monastics gather for the opening of the 18th Conference
Participants from over 30 countries in Seoul
Bhikshunis in the robes of many traditions
Plenary session at the Coex Convention Center
Sisters in the Dharma share experiences
A global sangha of Buddhist women

conferences.title: Buddhist Women Beyond Boundaries
conferences.subtitle: Interfaith, Interdependence, and Environment
conferences.about: Held entirely online during the pandemic, the 17th conference connected over 1,200 participants from 38 countries in seven working languages. Sessions explored interfaith dialogue, interdependence, and the environment — proving that the Sakyadhita community could gather and flourish across any boundary.

conferences.title: New Horizons in Buddhism
conferences.about: The 16th conference convened in the Blue Mountains of Australia — Sakyadhita's first gathering in Oceania. Participants explored new horizons for Buddhist practice, scholarship, and community in the West.

coverage.headline: Sakyadhita comes to the Blue Mountains
coverage.excerpt: Ahead of the 16th Sakyadhita International Conference, Buddhist women from around the world gathered in the Blue Mountains for retreat and reflection — the association's first conference in Australia.

photos.caption: Roshi Joan Halifax at the gathering

conferences.title: Contemporary Buddhist Women
conferences.subtitle: Contemplation, Cultural Exchange & Social Action
conferences.host: Centre of Buddhist Studies, University of Hong Kong
conferences.about: Hosted at the University of Hong Kong, the 15th conference brought together contemporary Buddhist women for contemplation, cultural exchange, and social action — bridging academic scholarship and lived practice.

coverage.headline: Photos from the 15th Sakyadhita International Conference on Buddhist Women
coverage.excerpt: Buddhist nuns and laywomen from around the world traveled to Hong Kong for the 15th conference — marking 30 years since the first gathering in Bodhgaya in 1987. Photos by Olivier Adam.

coverage.headline: Sakyadhita in Hong Kong 2017
coverage.excerpt: Ven. Thubten Jampa reflects on the 15th Sakyadhita Conference, which drew around 900 participants from around the world to celebrate Sakyadhita's 30-year anniversary at the University of Hong Kong.

photos.caption: Three Korean nuns take a selfie outside the University of Hong Kong
Tibetan nuns welcome Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo
Korean nuns at the Lotus Pond Temple, Lantau Island
Chinese nuns chant during the opening ceremony
Nuns from Vietnam visit the Tian Tan Buddha

conferences.title: Compassion & Social Justice
conferences.about: Set near the ancient temples of Yogyakarta, the 14th conference focused on compassion and social justice — examining how Buddhist women translate contemplative insight into action for the benefit of society.

conferences.title: Buddhism at the Grassroots
conferences.about: The 13th conference returned to India — to Vaishali, where Mahapajapati Gotami became the first Buddhist nun. Participants explored Buddhism at the grassroots, honoring the lay communities and local practitioners who sustain the tradition.

conferences.title: Leading to Liberation
conferences.about: The 12th conference gathered at Sathira-Dhammasathan in Bangkok, a renowned center of contemplative practice. The theme, Leading to Liberation, explored Buddhist women's paths of practice and realization.

conferences.loc: Universal Light Monastery · Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
conferences.title: Eminent Buddhist Women
conferences.about: Held in Ho Chi Minh City, the 11th conference celebrated eminent Buddhist women across history and the present day — honoring the teachers, scholars, and practitioners who have shaped the tradition.

conferences.title: Buddhism in Transition
conferences.subtitle: Tradition, Changes, & Challenges
conferences.about: The 10th conference convened in Ulaanbaatar — Sakyadhita's first gathering in Mongolia. Participants examined Buddhism in transition: the traditions, changes, and challenges facing practitioners in a rapidly modernizing world.

conferences.title: Buddhist Women in a Global Multicultural Community
conferences.about: Hosted in Kuala Lumpur, the 9th conference reflected on the place of Buddhist women within a global, multicultural community — drawing strength from diversity across traditions and nations.

conferences.title: Discipline & Practice of Buddhist Women
conferences.subtitle: Present & Past
conferences.about: The 8th conference gathered in Seoul to examine the discipline and practice of Buddhist women, present and past — with particular attention to the vinaya and the living monastic traditions of Korea.

conferences.title: Bridging Worlds
conferences.about: Held at Huafan University in Taipei, the 7th conference focused on bridging worlds — connecting Buddhist traditions, cultures, and generations of women across Asia and beyond.

conferences.title: Women as Peacemakers
conferences.subtitle: Self, Family, Community, World
conferences.about: At the millennium, the 6th conference gathered at Lumbini, the birthplace of the Buddha, with the theme Women as Peacemakers — exploring peace at every scale, from the self to the wider world.

conferences.title: Women in Buddhism: Unity & Diversity
conferences.about: The 5th conference convened at Wat Onaloum in Phnom Penh, exploring unity and diversity among Buddhist women — a gathering of resilience in a nation rebuilding its Buddhist institutions.

conferences.loc: Mahabodhi Meditation Center · Leh, Ladakh, India
conferences.title: Women & the Power of Compassion
conferences.subtitle: Survival in the 21st Century
conferences.about: High in the Himalayas at Leh, Ladakh, the 4th conference reflected on women and the power of compassion — and what compassion means for human survival in the coming century.

conferences.title: Buddhist Women in Modern Society
conferences.about: The 3rd conference gathered in Colombo, Sri Lanka, examining the role of Buddhist women in modern society — and laying groundwork for the restoration of the bhikkhuni order in the Theravada tradition.

conferences.title: Buddhist Women in the Modern World
conferences.about: The 2nd conference convened at Thammasat University in Bangkok, broadening the conversation on Buddhist women in the modern world and strengthening the international network founded four years earlier.

conferences.title: Buddhist Nuns in Society
conferences.host: Founders: Ayya Khema, Chatsumarn Kabilsingh, Karma Lekshe Tsomo
conferences.about: The very first Sakyadhita International Conference, held at the Kalachakra Temple in Bodhgaya, India — the site of the Buddha's awakening. Founded by Ayya Khema, Chatsumarn Kabilsingh, and Karma Lekshe Tsomo, it established Sakyadhita and named women's ordination as the movement's signature concern.

photos.caption: Opening ceremony of the International Conference on Buddhist Nuns · Bodhgaya, 1987
